imilar scenario for FY 2004-05, the shortfall would be $6.2 million. The second issue Chief Massetani addressed was overtime for field operations for FY 2004- 05. He noted that based on the projected retirements through June 30, 2004, the reactivation of Station 33 and the two H28 positions at the Division of Training, the Department will start the fiscal year understaffed by 74 uniformed personnel. The Department is currently budgeted for $3.6 million for overtime, which would fund approximately seven WDOs a day. The Department is projecting a $10.6 million shortfall in overtime even when taking into consideration

ocr_excerpts: ,374 annually in 2003 dollars. 1 Comments: 1. As part of its comprehensive management audit of the Fire Department, the Budget Analyst's Office reviewed the Fire Department's training and education functions. The Budget Analyst's Office concluded in Section 1.4 of the Management Audit Report that: • Fire Department training and education functions are currently spread across the Division of Training, the EMS Academy Section of the EMS Division, and the EMS In-service Training Section of the EMS Division. • Structural and management fragmentation hinders integration of the Fire Department's fire suppression and emergency medical services responsibilities, complicates management accountability, creates

implementing the Budget Analyst's recommendations in order to achieve an integrated Division of Fire and Medical Training by FY 2003-2004. According to Deputy Chief Asaro, the Fire Department: • Supports integration and consolidation of all training and education functions under the current name of the Division of Training once a common training facility is Board of Supervisors Budget Analyst 56 Memo to Finance Committee December 18, 2002 Finance Committee Meeting available instead of the current three training locations. • States that "a pool of candidates with both fire and medical training does not exist from the rank of H-30 Captain which is used to fill the H-39 Training Captain positions or in the H-33 Paramedic Captain which is used to fill the H-43 Section Chief positions." The Fire Department contends that the integrated Division of Training should support promotional opportunities for Fire Department staff who have the necessary experience in "site specific fire and emergency medical policies, procedures, and standards applicable to the San Francisco Fire Department." • Objects to a reduction in the number of officers responsible for in-se

Fire Department has failed to do so. According to Ms. Christine Ragan of the Fire Department, the approved emergency medical services merger plan never required the Fire Department to cross train Classification H-33 Rescue Captains. The Budget Analyst considers that cross-training of current and future Fire Department training and education providers, regardless of their classification, would provide role models to encourage more Fire Department staff to cross-train. While applicants for the Fire Department's training and education management positions from within the ranks of the Fire Department would have San Francisco Fire

Fazackerley Section Chief, Operations 2. PUBLIC COMMENT James Connors, Recruit Training Captain, Division of Training, wished to bring a matter before the Commission that affected his career and his family. He referred to a 12/7/05 letter by Kevin Smith, President, Black Firefighters Association, which accused him of creating a hostile environment and of being in violation of EEO policies. It was Mr. Connors' respo

onsibility to counsel all recruits and provide them with a clear picture of their training performance. Kevin Smith continues to http://www.sfgov.org/site/firecomm_page.asp?id=37361 2/27/2007 SFGo\ Fire Commission: January 12,2006 Page 2 of 16 use the word "racist" in his references to SFFD and the Division of Training. Mr. Connors requested that the Commission take steps to have Kevin Smith desist from using this type of harmful and inflammatory language, as he was already directed to do by the Department of Human Resources. Dr Fred Chavana. Chair, Department of Administrative Justice and Fire Science, City Colle

claims mad against him. He has never displayed any derogatory behavior against anyone or any group in discharging his duties or in any personal interactions. Joel Sato. President. Asian Firefighters Association, stated that his organization is in full support of Captain Connors and his staff at the Division of Training. He is an example of professionalism and sets an example for equal treatment among all the people he works with, including recruits. Personally, Mr. Sato believed that nothing destroys the good work done in the name of civil rights and affirmative action than the false allegations of racism recently
